user_prompt: I went through `lib\ESP-FTP-Server-Lib` and `src\tests\test_webserver.cpp` to swap out `FS` with `SdFs` and also `File` with `FsFile`
Go through and correct all the mistakes. Do not build
agent_reply: I cleaned up the `SdFs` / `FsFile` conversion mistakes without building.

The main fixes are in [FTPFilesystem.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.h:8) and [FTPFilesystem.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.cpp:22):
- Switched `open()` from Arduino string modes to SdFat `oflag_t` flags with `O_RDONLY`.
- Removed the old virtual-root implementation based on Arduino `fs::FileImpl` and `File`, since that cannot produce an `FsFile`.
- Fixed the bad `fs::SdFs` namespace leftover.
- Made the single mounted `SdFs` act as the FTP root, while still tolerating paths prefixed with the mount name.
I fixed the command handlers’ `FsFile` API assumptions too:
- Added a shared `FsFile::getName(...)` helper in [FTPCommand.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPCommand.h:95).

- Replaced `File::name()` usage in [LIST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/LIST.h:27), [NLST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/NLST.h:27), [STAT.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STAT.h:23), and [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:64).
- Replaced `getLastWrite()` in [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:49) with SdFat modification date/time fields.
- Replaced `"w"` in [STOR.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STOR.h:24) with `O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC`, so uploads create or overwrite as FTP `STOR` expects.
I also removed the unused chip-select constant from [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:12).

One behavior note: because the Arduino virtual mount-root no longer fits `FsFile`, the current single microSD mount is exposed directly at FTP `/` instead of showing a synthetic `/microSD` directory first. That matches the current wrapper use case, which serves only the microSD card. I did not build, per your instruction.
I cleaned up the `SdFs` / `FsFile` conversion mistakes without building.

The main fixes are in [FTPFilesystem.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.h:8) and [FTPFilesystem.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPFilesystem.cpp:22):
- Switched `open()` from Arduino string modes to SdFat `oflag_t` flags with `O_RDONLY`.
- Removed the old virtual-root implementation based on Arduino `fs::FileImpl` and `File`, since that cannot produce an `FsFile`.
- Fixed the bad `fs::SdFs` namespace leftover.
- Made the single mounted `SdFs` act as the FTP root, while still tolerating paths prefixed with the mount name.
I fixed the command handlers’ `FsFile` API assumptions too:
- Added a shared `FsFile::getName(...)` helper in [FTPCommand.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPCommand.h:95).

- Replaced `File::name()` usage in [LIST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/LIST.h:27), [NLST.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/NLST.h:27), [STAT.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STAT.h:23), and [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:64).
- Replaced `getLastWrite()` in [MLSD.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/MLSD.h:49) with SdFat modification date/time fields.
- Replaced `"w"` in [STOR.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/STOR.h:24) with `O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC`, so uploads create or overwrite as FTP `STOR` expects.
I also removed the unused chip-select constant from [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:12).

One behavior note: because the Arduino virtual mount-root no longer fits `FsFile`, the current single microSD mount is exposed directly at FTP `/` instead of showing a synthetic `/microSD` directory first. That matches the current wrapper use case, which serves only the microSD card. I did not build, per your instruction.

user_prompt: I'm testing it, the login worked, it's having trouble listing directory
[ 49998][I][NetworkClient.cpp:261] connect(): select returned due to timeout 3000 ms for fd 50
agent_reply: Your log says the directory listing failure is **FTP data-channel mode**, not the SdFat listing code.
The key sequence is:
```text
PASV
500 Unknown command
MLSD
... connect(): select returned due to timeout ...

This library had no passive mode. Your FTP client tried `PASV`, got rejected, fell back to active mode with `PORT`, then the device tried to open a data connection back to your client at `192.168.4.2:24702`. That callback connection timed out, so `MLSD` never got a data channel to send the directory listing on.
I added minimal passive-mode support:
- New `PASV` command in [PASV.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/Commands/PASV.h:1)
- Passive data state in [FTPConnection.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPConnection.h:11)
- Existing `LIST`, `MLSD`, `NLST`, upload, and download paths now accept the inbound passive data socket through [FTPCommand.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPCommand.h:32)
For this server’s existing single-control-connection design, I used one fixed passive data port: `50009`.
I also updated the vendored library README because it no longer accurately said “active mode only” after this patch: [README.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/README.md:31).
I ran `pio run -e thefly` after the change:
- The FTP library changes compiled successfully.
- The build still fails only at the already-known link step with the same IRAM overflow by `2920` bytes.
After flashing this change, the happy-path log should look more like:
```text
PASV
MLSD
with `PASV` returning `227 ...`, and it should **not** need to fall back to `PORT`.
Also, these lines are expected for this library:
```text
AUTH TLS
AUTH SSL
It is still plain FTP, so it does not support TLS, FTPS, or SFTP.

user_prompt: see the previous git commit, did I do anything wrong in the merge resolution?
agent_reply: Yes, I found two likely merge-resolution mistakes.
**Findings**

- High: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:557) registers `request->onDisconnect([] { endWebUpload(); })`, then [line 586](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:586) registers another `onDisconnect` for `cancelUploadFile(session_id)`. In this ESPAsyncWebServer, `onDisconnect()` is a setter, so the second callback overwrites the first. If an upload disconnects after the file is opened, `g_web_upload_active` can stay true forever, and [guiShouldYield](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.cpp:115) will keep the GUI yielding. Fix by using one combined callback that cancels the upload and ends web upload.

- Medium: [index.html](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/index.html:45) kept `<a class="delete-link" href="/delete_file?file_name=">`, but [script.js](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:249) treats it like a button: sets `disabled`, assigns `onclick`, and does a `fetch(POST)` without `preventDefault()`. Clicking can still follow the stale empty `href`, potentially navigating away or racing/canceling the POST. Best fix is to restore these to `<button type="button" class="delete-link">`, matching the JS side.
**Other Checks**

No unresolved conflict markers found. `node --check html/script.js` passes. `git diff --check HEAD^1 HEAD` reports trailing whitespace in `lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPCommand.h`, but that looks like imported/vendor code rather than the conflict area.
I did not change files; this was review-only.
Yes, I found two likely merge-resolution mistakes.
**Findings**

- High: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:557) registers `request->onDisconnect([] { endWebUpload(); })`, then [line 586](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:586) registers another `onDisconnect` for `cancelUploadFile(session_id)`. In this ESPAsyncWebServer, `onDisconnect()` is a setter, so the second callback overwrites the first. If an upload disconnects after the file is opened, `g_web_upload_active` can stay true forever, and [guiShouldYield](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/AsyncFsManager.cpp:115) will keep the GUI yielding. Fix by using one combined callback that cancels the upload and ends web upload.

- Medium: [index.html](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/index.html:45) kept `<a class="delete-link" href="/delete_file?file_name=">`, but [script.js](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:249) treats it like a button: sets `disabled`, assigns `onclick`, and does a `fetch(POST)` without `preventDefault()`. Clicking can still follow the stale empty `href`, potentially navigating away or racing/canceling the POST. Best fix is to restore these to `<button type="button" class="delete-link">`, matching the JS side.
**Other Checks**

No unresolved conflict markers found. `node --check html/script.js` passes. `git diff --check HEAD^1 HEAD` reports trailing whitespace in `lib/ESP-FTP-Server-Lib/src/FTPCommand.h`, but that looks like imported/vendor code rather than the conflict area.
I did not change files; this was review-only.
